care.summary: A popular, long-lived trailing houseplant known for its thick, waxy leaves and star-shaped, fragrant flower clusters.
care.light: Bright, indirect light. Can tolerate some morning sun. Too little light prevents blooming.
care.water: Allow the top half of the soil to dry out between waterings. Drought-tolerant due to succulent leaves.
care.soil: Very well-draining, chunky mix. A combination of potting soil, orchid bark, and perlite is ideal.
care.humidity: Prefers moderate to high humidity (40-60%), but adapts well to average household humidity.
care.fertilizer: Diluted balanced liquid fertilizer once a month during spring and summer.
tips: Do not cut off the long leafless tendrils; leaves and flower spurs develop on them.
Do not remove the peduncles (flower stalks) after blooming, as they reflower from the same spot.
